title: DETACH FROM MAYA & ATTACH TO DIVINE | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Hare Krishna. A warm welcome to all of you for today's Gita Live session.
We have a very interesting topic today and the topic is
Detach from Maya and Attach to Divine.
We all know that we are trapped in Maya.
All of us are in Maya. The degree may vary.
And we have all heard this people saying quite often
Hum toh Maya jaal mein phasay hoon hai.
So today we will understand more in detail what is this Maya jaal.
What are the ways in which Maya operates and traps the living entity?
And eventually we will also discuss how to come out of this Maya jaal.
So before we plunge into this Maya jaal,
before we plunge into the discussion, let us all pray together.
I request all of you to kindly fold your hands and join me in offering the prayers.
The prayers will be displayed on the screen and we can all chant the prayers together.
Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale
Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare
So today we will understand how maya traps a living entity and why it does so.
Why we fall into the traps of maya.
Maya is an energy of the Lord.
Devi Esha Gunamayi Mamma Maya Dhurateya.
It is my energy, my divine energy.
On one side we say that we are trapped in the trap of maya.
On the other side we say it is the energy of Krishna.
It looks a little bit of contradiction.
On one side we see maya as a villain.
On the other side we say it is an energy of the Lord.
So how do we understand this?
The right understanding is just like a policeman, a person who is maintaining law and order,
But for a criminal, for a person who has transgressed the law,
that person can be very notorious, can be very painful to deal with,
jivira krishna bhulia jiva bhoga vancha kare.
When we forget the Lord and desire to enjoy,
keeping Lord aside, make our independent plans of enjoyment,
krishna bhulia jiva bhoga vancha kare.
maya immediately pounces on him and traps him.
Just like you have traffic laws, you have traffic lights,
you will have the police chasing you and penalizing you.
So long as you are abiding by the laws, the police will not disturb you.
In fact, the police is there to maintain law and order.
But moment somebody transgresses that law, violates that law,
the police department will come and nab that person and punish that person.
Exactly the same role maya is playing.
And in one sense, all of us have come in this material world.
Now, some of us may ask this question.
Do you mean to say we have committed some crime?
That we are trapped by maya?
We can be compared to criminals.
Criminals are the people who have violated the laws of the land.
So likewise, there are laws which are operating in this world.
jivera swaroopa hoye nityera krishna das.
By swaroopa, by our constitutional position,
we are meant to follow and abide by the instructions,
And we also get our due court of enjoyment.
This example I have shared many, many times.
Just like, now does that mean that this is not fair?
We should just give enjoyment to the Lord?
What if we enjoy legally, properly, lawfully?
Yes, legal enjoyment has a place.
In fact, to enjoy is the goal of life.
A living entity by nature is joyful.
But we have been designed just like this limbs of the body.
When they work for the interest of the body,
they get their due court of enjoyment.
Hand cannot enjoy rasgulla independently on its own
by squeezing the rasgulla and trying to extract the juice out of it.
The hand has to play its role.
It is designed to put the food in the mouth,
and the hand gets its due court of nourishment.
This is how it is designed.
Each limb is playing a particular role.
The legs walk, the hands write, the eyes see,
and body as a whole is designed.
And that's how the whole body functions nicely and does wonderful things.
So when a particular limb rebels and says,
no, no, I will independently enjoy.
Some similar case has happened when the jiva tries to enjoy independently.
maya jhapatiya dhare comes under the influence of maya.
For a person who is abiding by the laws diligently,
police department has no role to play.
Will not trouble such a person.
Likewise, till we come to our senses,
In fact, it is said that the devotees of the Lord and maya
are actually playing the same role,

nab the person who has violated the law and penalize him.

till he comes to the senses and eventually comes

and your life will be sublime.
there is a common role being played by maya

maya acts on the living entity,
and why we get, so to say,

we have become servants of our senses,
of being servant of the Lord,

makes us forget our original position.

It is said that in the animal kingdom,
for each of the senses there is an example given
which eventually even loses its life
as far as that particular sense is concerned.
For example, the taste, the tongue, the example is given of a fish. Even though it is not hungry,
but whenever a fisherman goes and throws a hook with a nice bait dangling, the fish helplessly
goes for that bait and eventually gets caught, trapped and gets killed.
Another example, the deer. Whenever the hunter goes to the forest to hunt for deers,
because the deers run very very fast, it's not easy to chase them down and kill them.
But the hunter knows the weakness. The hunter knows that the deer gets completely
plays very melodious music, the deer almost becomes numb, stays put in a place
just like a duck, does not move and it's very very easy for the hunter to shoot the deer down.
We have seen wild elephants, especially the untamed ones.
If you have to capture that elephant, wild elephant and tame it,
they have realized it's very difficult because elephants are huge.
For a man who is 70 kg, 80 kg, it's not easy to capture that wild elephant.
But they have learned that when the she-elephant who is trained
passes by that male elephant and touches the body at a particular spot, the male elephant gets aroused
and madly starts chasing the elephant.
And the she-elephant is trained, it passes in a particular way and passes across
by the side of a pit which is deep pit covered with green grass.
And as soon as this male elephant madly is chasing that she-elephant,
he falls on that pit and eventually gets trapped and then beaten till it becomes tamed.
And then we have example of musk.
Now we have this perfume musk. It comes from musk deer,
where musk is there and because of that enchanting smell.
The sense of smell, the nose, the deer runs looking out for that smell,
the source of that smell and runs
right from the mountains to the mountains.
mountains to deep forests, deep deserts, looking where is that smell coming from, what is the
source of that smell, not knowing that it's within its body itself.
And keep searching, searching, searching, running behind that smell and eventually dies.
And then we have example of a moth.
The moths, especially winter times when there is light, they think that it's a flame, burning
flame, dancing flame, attracts the moth, gives them warmth, they hover near that light and
eventually the heat starts increasing, it becomes warm and warmer.
Eventually it burns that moth and next day morning if you see it, all the moths would
The same light which was attracting that moth.
The moth helplessly becomes the cause of its death.
So our scriptures say that we have examples that how, the examples which I shared, one
of the senses being weak becomes the cause of death of that particular animal.
But in the case of a man, a human being, it's not one sense, it's all our senses.
Which have gone astray, which are weak, by which we get tempted.
And this is how the Maya acts, instead of acting as per the original design of being
servant of the Lord, Krishna Das, we have become slaves of our senses, slaves of our
We have become slaves of our senses, slaves of our eyes, ears, tongue.

And if we analyze all the lifestyle diseases, all the challenges we face, all the problems
we have encountered, all the suffering, it's all because of uncontrolled mind, uncontrolled
And this is how Maya has trapped us.

to that man if you give a drop of water, it's not going to quench his thirst.

But this very happiness which we get by bringing the senses in contact with the sense objects,
this very happiness becomes the cause of our distress, our misery, our entanglement.
Therefore, all the scriptures of the world talk about regulating the senses, controlling
Mind if it is controlled is a best friend.
The same mind if it is uncontrolled is a worst enemy.
From the very beginning, regulate your senses.

By our own efforts, by logic, by rational understanding, we gave example of that bird.
